message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #2 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: pip-audit CI job omits --strict, so silently-skipped (unauditable) deps pass the supply-chain gate
DIMENSION: dependency & supply chain
SEVERITY: HIGH
FILE: .github/workflows/ci.yml 89-95
DESCRIPTION: The audit job's stated purpose is to "fail on known-vulnerable dependencies (a security/privacy engine must not ship with advisories)". But the invocation `pip-audit --progress-spinner off $IGNORES` does NOT pass `--strict`/`-S`. I confirmed in the venv (.venv/bin/pip-audit 2.10.1) that without --strict pip-audit exits 0 even when a dependency cannot be collected/audited, and exits 1 with --strict: `pip-audit --progress-spinner off` -> exit 0; `pip-audit --progress-spinner off --strict` -> exit 1. The job ALREADY hits this: every run prints `assay-engine ... Dependency not found on PyPI and could not be audited` and still passes. The same silent-skip occurs for any transitive package that fails advisory-DB collection (a transient OSV/PyPI hiccup, a yanked/private package, or a future dep not on PyPI). Result: a dependency can go completely un-scanned while CI reports green, defeating the gate. For a security-branded engine this is a real coverage hole, not style.
SUGGESTED FIX: Add `-S`/`--strict` to the pip-audit invocation so any failure to collect/audit a dependency fails the job. If the local `assay-engine` self-package must be excluded, audit a generated requirements/lockfile of the third-party deps instead of the live env (see lockfile finding), rather than tolerating arbitrary silent skips.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this finding by examining the CI workflow and testing pip-audit behavior.

content.content:
1	name: ci
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [main]
6	  pull_request:
7	
8	jobs:
9	  # Dependency-free core: the engine must lint, type-check, and test with NO extras installed
10	  # (optional backends are import-guarded; integration tests skip). This guards ADR-0006.
11	  core:
12	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
13	    strategy:
14	      matrix:
15	        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12", "3.13", "3.14"]
16	    steps:
17	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
18	      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
19	        with:
20	          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
21	      - name: Install (no extras)
22	        run: |
23	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
24	          pip install -e ".[dev]"
25	      - name: Lint (ruff)
26	        run: ruff check src tests
27	      - name: Format check (ruff)
28	        run: ruff format --check src tests
29	      - name: Type-check (mypy)
30	        run: mypy
31	      - name: Test (pytest)
32	        run: pytest -q
33	
34	  # Extras lane: install the real backends and run the integration suite so the backend code
35	  # paths actually execute, and mypy type-checks against the REAL (not ignored) library types.
36	  # Service-dependent tests (ollama/qdrant/postgres) skip when the service is absent in CI; the
37	  # extra-only tests (langgraph graph, mlflow lifecycle, OTel, numpy primitives, methodology
38	  # e2e) run for real.
39	  integration:
40	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
41	    services:
42	      postgres:
43	        image: postgres:16
44	        env:
45	          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
46	          POSTGRES_DB: assay
47	        ports: ["5432:5432"]
48	        options: >-
49	          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U postgres"
50	          --health-interval 5s --health-timeout 5s --health-retries 10
51	      qdrant:
52	        image: qdrant/qdrant:v1.12.4
53	        ports: ["6333:6333"]
54	    env:
55	      # loopback host keeps the data-sovereignty guard satisfied; creds are redacted on error
56	      ASSAY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/assay
57	    steps:
58	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
59	      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
60	        with:
61	          python-version: "3.12"
62	      - name: Install (all extras)
63	        run: |
64	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
65	          pip install -e ".[dev,reasoning,observability,persistence,orchestration,baseline]"
66	      - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
67	        run: mypy
68	      # Real backends now run: langgraph graph + interrupt/resume, mlflow lifecycle, OTel,
69	      # numpy primitives, the T3 drain/classification (synthetic SDK, no metered call), AND —
70	      # via the service containers — the live Postgres checkpointer + Qdrant vector store.
71	      # Ollama and the metered frontier tier are not in CI and skip honestly.
72	      - name: Integration + unit tests with coverage
73	        run: pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing
74	
75	  # Supply-chain: fail on known-vulnerable dependencies (a security/privacy engine must not ship
76	  # with advisories). An allowlist file (.pip-audit-ignore, one advisory id per line) covers
77	  # accepted/unfixable ones. (#108)
78	  audit:
79	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
80	    steps:
81	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
82	      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
83	        with:
84	          python-version: "3.12"
85	      - name: Install (all extras) + pip-audit
86	        run: |
87	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip pip-audit
88	          pip install -e ".[dev,all]"
89	      - name: pip-audit (known-vulnerability scan)
90	        run: |
91	          IGNORES=""
92	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
93	            while read -r id; do [ -n "$id" ] && IGNORES="$IGNORES --ignore-vuln $id"; done < .pip-audit-ignore
94	          fi
95	          pip-audit --progress-spinner off $IGNORES
96

content.text: The finding is confirmed in the source. Let me verify the pip-audit behavior in the venv.

input.command: cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && ls -la .pip-audit-ignore 2>&1; echo "---"; cat .pip-audit-ignore 2>&1 || echo "no file"

content.content:
ls: .pip-audit-ignore: No such file or directory
---
cat: .pip-audit-ignore: No such file or directory
no file

content.text: Let me reproduce the core claim: does pip-audit exit 0 when a dep can't be audited, and exit 1 with --strict?

input.command: cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/pip-audit --progress-spinner off >/tmp/audit_nostrict.log 2>&1; echo "NO-STRICT exit: $?"; echo "==="; grep -iE "could not be audited|not found on PyPI|skip" /tmp/audit_nostrict.log | head -20

content.content:
NO-STRICT exit: 0
===
Name         Skip Reason
assay-engine Dependency not found on PyPI and could not be audited: assay-engine (0.0.1)

input.command: cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/pip-audit --progress-spinner off --strict >/tmp/audit_strict.log 2>&1; echo "STRICT exit: $?"; echo "==="; tail -5 /tmp/audit_strict.log

content.content:
STRICT exit: 1
===
ERROR:pip_audit._cli:assay-engine: Dependency not found on PyPI and could not be audited: assay-engine (0.0.1)

content.text:
Both claims reproduced exactly:
- Without `--strict`: exit 0, despite `assay-engine` being silently skipped (not audited).
- With `--strict`: exit 1.

Now let me confirm the editable self-package `assay-engine` is the package being installed (confirming the CI env would have the same skip), and check whether there's any compensating mechanism.

input.command: cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && grep -E "^name|^version" pyproject.toml | head -5; echo "---grep for strict/pip-audit in repo---"; grep -rEn "pip-audit|--strict|-S " .github/ Makefile* 2>/dev/null | grep -iv "spinner" | head; echo "---lockfile check---"; ls -la requirements*.txt *.lock uv.lock poetry.lock 2>/dev/null || echo "no lockfile"
input.description: Check package name and lockfile presence

content.content:
name = "assay-engine"
---grep for strict/pip-audit in repo---
(eval):1: no matches found: Makefile*
---lockfile check---
(eval):1: no matches found: requirements*.txt
no lockfile

content.text:
The finding is fully reproduced and accurate. Let me assess the practical severity by considering how the gate behaves in CI.

The package being installed via `pip install -e ".[dev,all]"` is `assay-engine` itself (editable, version 0.0.1), which is not on PyPI. So **every CI audit run** hits at least one silent skip (the self-package), confirming the assessor's claim that "the job ALREADY hits this." More importantly, without `--strict`, any transitive third-party dep that fails advisory-DB collection (transient OSV/PyPI outage, yanked package, future private dep) is silently skipped while CI reports green — a genuine coverage hole in the stated supply-chain gate.

Let me verify the severity calibration: the self-package skip is benign in itself (an editable local package legitimately has no PyPI advisories), but the mechanism that allows it also allows real third-party deps to be skipped silently. The suggested fix (`--strict`) is correct but would require excluding the self-package — confirming the assessor's nuance that you'd want to audit a generated requirements set or use `--strict` with proper scoping.

input.description: Check pip-audit flags for editable handling

content.content:
[--fix] [--require-hashes] [--index-url INDEX_URL]
                 [--extra-index-url URL] [--skip-editable] [--no-deps]
                 [-o FILE] [--ignore-vuln ID] [--disable-pip]
                 [-r REQUIREMENT | --path PATH | project_path]
--
  -S, --strict          fail the entire audit if dependency collection fails
                        on any dependency (default: False)
  --desc [{on,off,auto}]
--
  --path PATH           restrict to the specified installation path for
                        auditing packages; this option can be used multiple
                        times (default: [])
--
  --require-hashes      require a hash to check each requirement against, for
                        repeatable audits; this option is implied when any
                        package in a requirements file has a `--hash` option.
--
  --skip-editable       don't audit packages that are marked as editable
                        (default: False)
  --no-deps             don't perform any dependency resolution; requires all

content.text:
This confirms the complete picture. `pip-audit` has both `--strict` (fail on any collection failure) and `--skip-editable` (to legitimately exclude the editable self-package). The correct fix is `pip-audit --strict --skip-editable ...`, which would fail on real un-auditable third-party deps while not tripping on the local editable `assay-engine`. The assessor's diagnosis and suggested fix are correct.

The finding is real. The `-S, --strict` help text literally describes the gap: without it, "dependency collection fail[ure] on any dependency" does not fail the audit.

Assessment:
- The bug is real and reproduced exactly as described (exit 0 without strict, exit 1 with strict; the self-package skip occurs on every run).
- This is a CI/supply-chain gate coverage hole, not a runtime defect. Impact is conditional: it only hides a real vulnerability when a genuinely vulnerable third-party dep also fails advisory-DB collection (transient outage, yanked/private package, or future non-PyPI dep). The current self-package skip is itself benign.
- Severity: the assessor said HIGH. I'd calibrate to MEDIUM. The gate is weakened and unauditable deps can pass green, which is a legitimate defect for a security-branded engine, but it does not by itself ship a known vulnerability — it only fails to catch one in a non-default/degraded scenario. The probability of a silent miss is real but not the common case (today's only skip is the inert local package). That weighs against HIGH but clearly above LOW, since the gate's stated guarantee ("must not ship with advisories") is not actually enforced under collection failures.

input.reasoning:
CONFIRMED TRUE. The pip-audit invocation at .github/workflows/ci.yml:89-95 (`pip-audit --progress-spinner off $IGNORES`) omits `-S`/`--strict`, so any dependency whose advisory data cannot be collected is silently skipped while the job still exits 0. The job's stated purpose (lines 75-77) is to "fail on known-vulnerable dependencies (a security/privacy engine must not ship with advisories)", so silently un-scanned deps passing green is a genuine coverage hole in the stated gate, not style.

Reproduced both assertions in the project venv (pip-audit 2.10.1):
- `pip-audit --progress-spinner off` -> exit 0, with `assay-engine Dependency not found on PyPI and could not be audited: assay-engine (0.0.1)` in the Skip Reason table.
- `pip-audit --progress-spinner off --strict` -> exit 1, same dep reported as ERROR.
The pip-audit --help text confirms semantics: `-S, --strict  fail the entire audit if dependency collection fails on any dependency (default: False)`.

The assessor's claim that the CI job ALREADY hits this is correct: CI installs `pip install -e ".[dev,all]"`, and the editable self-package is `assay-engine` (pyproject name = assay-engine, version 0.0.1) which is not on PyPI, so every audit run produces at least one silent skip. The same silent-skip path applies to any third-party/transitive package that fails advisory-DB collection (transient OSV/PyPI outage, yanked/private package, or a future non-PyPI dep), meaning a genuinely vulnerable dep could go un-scanned while CI is green.

The suggested fix is sound and the tooling supports a clean implementation: `pip-audit -S --skip-editable --progress-spinner off $IGNORES` would fail on un-auditable third-party deps while legitimately excluding the editable local package (avoiding a false failure on the inert self-package).

NOT a pass-1 re-report: pass-1 #108 added the supply-chain audit job + .pip-audit-ignore allowlist, but did not address strict collection-failure handling; this is a residual gap that #108 missed, not a regression.

Severity recalibrated from HIGH to MEDIUM: the gate is weakened and unauditable deps can pass green, which is a real defect for a security-branded engine, but it is a CI gate-coverage hole, not a runtime vulnerability, and it only hides a real advisory in a degraded/transient scenario (a vulnerable dep must ALSO fail collection). Today's only actual skip is the inert local editable package, which is harmless. That conditional, non-default impact places it above LOW but below HIGH.
input.evidence: File .github/workflows/ci.yml lines 89-95 — `pip-audit --progress-spinner off $IGNORES` lacks `-S`/`--strict`. CI install line 88 `pip install -e ".[dev,all]"` makes the editable self-package `assay-engine` (pyproject name=assay-engine, version 0.0.1, not on PyPI) a guaranteed silent skip every run. Venv repro (.venv/bin/pip-audit 2.10.1): without --strict -> exit 0 with `assay-engine ... Dependency not found on PyPI and could not be audited`; with --strict -> exit 1 (ERROR). pip-audit --help: `-S, --strict  fail the entire audit if dependency collection fails on any dependency (default: False)` and `--skip-editable  don't audit packages that are marked as editable`. No lockfile/requirements*.txt present (audit runs against the live env), so any collection failure is tolerated. Suggested fix: `pip-audit -S --skip-editable --progress-spinner off $IGNORES`.

content.text: Verified TRUE. The pip-audit CI job at `/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.github/workflows/ci.yml:89-95` omits `--strict`, so deps that fail advisory-DB collection are silently skipped while the job exits 0. Reproduced both claims in the venv (pip-audit 2.10.1): exit 0 without `--strict` (with `assay-engine` skipped), exit 1 with `--strict`. Recalibrated severity HIGH -> MEDIUM (CI gate-coverage hole conditional on a real dep also failing collection; today's only skip is the inert editable self-package). Suggested fix: `pip-audit -S --skip-editable ...`.
